statement.markdown: Write a code to perform primality test on a given integer. A primality test is a method to check whether a number is prime or composite. For example, input 5 should return True, while input 6 should return False.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer n.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a boolean value indicating whether the number is prime or not.
statement.notes: You can use trial division or any other suitable method for the primality test. However, be aware of potential issues with very large inputs.

constraints.description: Input integer to check for primality

hints.text: Check for edge cases, like input 1 or 2.
Understand the difference between trial division and other methods for primality test.

editorial.title: Primality Test Editorial
editorial.intuition: Use trial division method to check for factors.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through all numbers up to sqrt(n).
editorial.betterSolution: Use Pollard's rho algorithm or similar optimizations.
editorial.optimalSolution: Apply Miller-Rabin primality test for large inputs.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Use Euclid's Lemma to prove primality.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run using trial division.
